back_translation: Therefore, since we have already been (royally) reckoned righteous by faith, we have peace with God through Jesus Christ our Lord
Through Him, we have gained access into this grace by faith — and we stand firm in this grace — and we joyfully rejoice in the hope of the (royal) glory of God
Not only that — we also joyfully rejoice in afflictions, because we know that affliction produces perseverance
And perseverance produces tested-character, and tested-character produces hope
And hope does not disappoint — for the love of God has been (royally) poured into our heart-minds, through the Holy (royal) Spirit who has been (royally) given to us already
For while we were still weak, Christ died for the ungodly at the appropriate time
For there is hardly anyone who would consent to die for a righteous person — but for a good person, perhaps someone might dare to die for him
But God (royally) demonstrates His love toward us in this — that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us
Therefore, since we have now been (royally) reckoned righteous by His (royal) blood, how much more shall we be saved from (royal) wrath through Him
For if while we were enemies, we were (royally) reconciled to God through the death of His Son, how much more, having been (royally) reconciled, shall we be saved by His (royal) life
Not only that — we also joyfully rejoice in God through Jesus Christ our Lord — through whom we have now received the reconciliation
Therefore, just as sin entered into the world through one man, and death entered through sin, so death spread to all humans, because all humans sinned
For before the time of receiving the Law, sin was already in the world — but sin is not counted-on-the-account when there is no Law
Nevertheless, death reigned from Adam until Moses — even over those who did not sin in the same pattern as Adam's transgression — who is the figure of the One who was to come

But the gift is not like the transgression — for if by the transgression of one man, the many died, how much more did the grace of God and the gift through the grace of one man, namely Jesus Christ, overflow to the many

And the gift is not like the result from the sin of one man — for the (royal) judgment began from one sin to the verdict of condemnation, but the gift began from many transgressions to (royal) reckoning as righteous

For if by the transgression of one man, death reigned through that one man, how much more will those who receive the overflowing grace and the gift of righteousness reign in life through one man — namely Jesus Christ
Therefore, just as one transgression brought the verdict of condemnation upon all humans, so also one act of righteousness brought (royal) reckoning as righteous and life upon all humans
For just as by the disobedience of one man, the many were appointed to be sinners, so also by the obedience of one man, the many will be (royally) appointed to be righteous
The Law slipped in alongside, so that the transgression would multiply — but where sin multiplied, grace overflowed even more abundantly
So that, just as sin reigned in death, so also grace will reign through righteousness, to eternal life, through Jesus Christ our Lord
